breaking_news.title: Interlock Ransomware Exploits Cisco Zero-Day in Active Attacks
breaking_news.summary:
The Interlock ransomware gang has been actively exploiting a critical zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-20131) in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center software since late January. This maximum-severity flaw allows unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary code and gain root access to the firewall management system.

Amazon security researchers confirmed that criminals abused this bug weeks before Cisco patched it, leading to widespread ransomware attacks against enterprise networks. This incident highlights the severe risk of unpatched infrastructure vulnerabilities in critical security appliances and the speed at which threat actors weaponize zero-days.

breaking_news.title: DarkSword iOS Exploit Kit Used by State-Sponsored Hackers
breaking_news.summary:
Google Threat Intelligence Group identified a new iOS exploit chain dubbed DarkSword that leverages six zero-day vulnerabilities to fully compromise iPhone devices. The exploit kit is being utilized by multiple commercial surveillance vendors and suspected state-sponsored actors, including the Russia-linked UNC6353 group.

DarkSword enables attackers to steal sensitive data, including cryptocurrency wallet information, and deploy malware families like GHOSTBLADE without user interaction. This proliferation of a sophisticated exploit chain across disparate threat actors mirrors previous campaigns and poses a significant threat to mobile device security globally.

breaking_news.title: GlassWorm Supply Chain Attack Compromises GitHub and VSCode
breaking_news.summary:
A coordinated supply chain attack known as GlassWorm has compromised hundreds of code repositories and extensions on GitHub, npm, and VSCode/OpenVSX. Threat actors used stolen GitHub tokens to force-push malicious code into popular Python projects and developer tools, injecting malware into the software supply chain.

The campaign targets developers by posing as helpful utilities like linters and formatters, allowing malware loaders to spread through dependency abuse. This widespread compromise of trusted development platforms underscores the growing vulnerability of open-source ecosystems to sophisticated supply chain infiltration.

breaking_news.title: Stryker Cyberattack Wipes Devices via Microsoft Environment
breaking_news.summary:
Medical technology giant Stryker suffered a disruptive cyberattack attributed to the pro-Palestinian hacktivist group Handala, which remotely wiped tens of thousands of employee devices. The attack targeted the company's internal Microsoft environment, forcing offices across 79 countries to shut down and disrupting electronic ordering systems.

While Stryker confirmed its medical devices remain safe, the incident resulted in the exfiltration of approximately 50TB of corporate data and significant operational downtime. This event demonstrates the increasing capability of hacktivist groups to target critical healthcare infrastructure and cause widespread business disruption.

breaking_news.title: CISA Adds Microsoft SharePoint and Zimbra Flaws to KEV Catalog
breaking_news.summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added two actively exploited vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, affecting Microsoft SharePoint and Synacor Zimbra. The flaws, including a deserialization issue in SharePoint and a cross-site scripting flaw in Zimbra, are currently being weaponized in the wild.

CISA has ordered federal agencies to apply patches immediately, emphasizing the urgency of securing these widely deployed enterprise collaboration tools. The inclusion of these flaws in the KEV catalog signals a high level of active threat activity and mandates compliance for government and critical infrastructure entities.

breaking_news.title: Critical Unpatched Telnetd Flaw Enables Remote Code Execution
breaking_news.summary:
Researchers disclosed a critical unpatched vulnerability (CVE-2026-32746) in the GNU InetUtils telnet daemon that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to execute code with elevated privileges. The flaw, rated 9.8 on the CVSS scale, stems from an out-of-bounds write in the LINEMODE handler and affects all versions up to 2.7.

Although a patch is expected soon, the immediate risk remains high for any systems still utilizing the outdated Telnet protocol for remote access. This vulnerability represents a severe threat to legacy infrastructure and highlights the dangers of maintaining obsolete network services in modern environments.

breaking_news.title: AWS Bedrock AI Sandbox Flaw Allows Data Exfiltration
breaking_news.summary:
BeyondTrust researchers revealed a security flaw in AWS Bedrock AgentCore's Code Interpreter sandbox that permits data exfiltration via DNS queries. Despite being designed for isolation, the sandbox allows outbound DNS requests that attackers can exploit to establish covert communication channels and execute remote commands.

This finding challenges the security assumptions of agentic AI execution environments, showing that perimeter controls may be insufficient against sophisticated AI-driven attacks. AWS acknowledged the report, but the incident serves as a warning for enterprises relying on cloud-based AI agents for sensitive workflows.

breaking_news.title: UK Companies House Flaw Exposes Millions of Firm Details
breaking_news.summary:
The UK government agency Companies House confirmed a security flaw in its WebFiling service exposed the personal details of company directors and allowed users to alter records. The vulnerability was active since October 2025, potentially affecting millions of firms registered in the United Kingdom.

The agency was forced to take the service offline to rectify the issue, raising concerns about the integrity of corporate registries and the potential for identity theft. This breach highlights the critical need for robust security in government infrastructure that manages sensitive business and personal data.
